board_csa_architect.notes: Neon-native DR is architecturally sound — leverages managed PITR instead of self-managed WAL. S3 supplemental dumps provide vendor portability. Clean separation from aspirational L6-13 design. Phase K promotion justified — no pilot without DR.

board_ciso_cybersecurity.notes: AES-256 SSE-KMS encryption on all S3 dumps. Credentials via env vars only — Anti-AC explicitly bans script-embedded secrets. Neon at-rest encryption handles WAL. S3 versioning + lifecycle prevents accidental data destruction. No plaintext dumps ever. GDPR-ready backup strategy.

board_caio_fullstack.notes: No AI/VRAM impact — DR is pure ops. Neon branching is instant (milliseconds, copy-on-write) so pre-deploy snapshots add zero latency to the deploy pipeline. No model or inference changes.

board_vp_ops_devops.notes: 3AM test: dr-backup.sh is automated via cron — no human needed. dr-verify.sh provides alerting on stale backups. Three restore scenarios cover the realistic failure modes. Neon PITR removes complexity of self-managed WAL. Cost-efficient — Neon Pro includes PITR at no extra charge.

board_red_team.notes: Failure scenarios: (1) S3 bucket misconfiguration — public access or wrong KMS key → unencrypted dumps. Mitigate with bucket policy validation in dr-verify.sh. (2) Neon API key compromise — attacker could delete branches. Mitigate with branch protection + API key rotation schedule. (3) dr-restore.sh used carelessly overwrites production data — add --confirm-destructive flag with 10s countdown. (4) Stale backup goes unnoticed — dr-verify.sh must integrate with alerting (PagerDuty/Slack). Condition: Add destructive operation safeguard to restore script.
